name: vercel

shell_env_vars.name: VERCEL_TELEMETRY_DISABLED
shell_env_vars.disables: Vercel CLI telemetry for this process only — per-run override. Does NOT change the persisted `collectMetrics` setting; `vercel telemetry status` still reports the config value.

cli_commands.disables: Persist Vercel CLI telemetry opt-out (writes collectMetrics=false to com.vercel.cli/config.json — cross-platform; covers all future runs without needing the env var)

persistent_files.note: macOS: CLI global config. Holds collectMetrics setting after opt-out.
macOS: OAuth token. Verify permissions are 0600 — leaking this grants account access.
Linux XDG_DATA_HOME path for the same config file.
Linux XDG_DATA_HOME path for the auth token. Same 0600 guidance as macOS.
Windows path per Vercel's global-configuration docs (XDG-style under %APPDATA%\Roaming). Unverified locally — reported for completeness.
Windows auth token location. Same 0600 guidance applies once the file exists.

notes: Two documented opt-outs — both from `https://vercel.com/docs/cli/about-telemetry`. The subcommand `vercel telemetry disable` persists across runs; `VERCEL_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1` is per-run only (quoting the docs: 'This will only apply for runs where the environment variable is set and will not change your configured telemetry status.'). Apply the subcommand for durable opt-out and surface the env var as a shell-rc belt-and-suspenders entry.
`VERCEL_TELEMETRY_DEBUG=1` is debug-only (redirects events to stderr) and is NOT a supported opt-out — do not suggest it.

Pre-login behavior of `vercel telemetry disable` was not verified locally during authoring — on a fresh machine with no `auth.json`, the command may fail or may succeed by creating only `config.json`. If users report failure, consider adding a `vercel login` prerequisite check.

Related Vercel-owned tools with separate telemetry streams (NOT covered by this vendor): Next.js (`NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1` + `next telemetry disable`), Turborepo (`TURBO_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1` + `turbo telemetry disable`). Add as discrete vendor files only if users ask.
